briefdescription.para: No epsilon closure.
Epsilon closure before the transition.
Epsilon closure after the transition.
Epsilon closure before and after the transition.
Both original states have to be final.
At least one of the original states has to be final.

memberdef.type: std::is_same< bool_pack< true, Ts::value... >, bool_pack< Ts::value..., true > >
memberdef.name: conjunction

briefdescription.para: Check that for all values in a pack Ts are 'true'.
Check that all types in a sequence of parameters Ts are of type T.
Map of additional parameter name and value pairs.
A non-deterministic finite automaton.

detaileddescription.para: An epsilon symbol which is now defined as the maximal value of data type used for symbols.

briefdescription.para: Create alphabet from variadic number of NFAs given as arguments.

detaileddescription.para:
in] Nfas Type Nfa.
nfas
NFAs to create alphabet from.
Created alphabet.

briefdescription.para: Create alphabet from a vector of NFAs.

parameterdescription.para: Vector of NFAs to create alphabet from.

briefdescription.para: Create alphabet from a vector of NFAs.

parameterdescription.para: Vector of NFAs to create alphabet from.

briefdescription.para: Create alphabet from a vector of NFAs.

parameterdescription.para: Vector of pointers to NFAs to create alphabet from.

briefdescription.para: Create alphabet from a vector of NFAs.

parameterdescription.para: Vector of pointers to NFAs to create alphabet from.

briefdescription.para: Compute non-deterministic union.

detaileddescription.para:
Does not add epsilon transitions, just unites initial and final states.
Non-deterministic union of lhs and rhs.

briefdescription.para: Compute union of two complete deterministic NFAs.

detaileddescription.para:
The union is computed by product construction with OR condition on the final states.
lhs
First complete deterministic automaton.
rhs
Second complete deterministic automaton.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Nfa &lhs, const Nfa &rhs, ProductFinalStateCondition final_condition=ProductFinalStateCondition::AND, Symbol first_epsilon=EPSILON, std::unordered_map< std::pair< State, State >, State > *prod_map=nullptr)
memberdef.name: product

briefdescription.para: Compute product of two NFAs with OR condition on the final states.

detaileddescription.para:
Automata must share alphabets. //TODO: this is not implemented yet.
lhs
First NFA.
rhs
Second NFA.
final_condition
Condition for a product state to be final.
AND: both original states have to be final.
OR: at least one of the original states has to be final.
first_epsilon
Smallest epsilon symbol. //TODO: this should eventually be taken from the alphabet as anything larger than the largest symbol?
prod_map
Mapping of pairs of the original states (lhs_state, rhs_state) to new product states (not used internally, allocated only when !=nullptr, expensive).

memberdef.argsstring: (const Nfa &nfa_included, const Nfa &nfa_excluded, std::optional< std::function< bool(const Nfa &, const Nfa &, const StateSet &, const StateSet &, const State, const Nfa &)> > macrostate_discover=std::nullopt)
memberdef.name: lang_difference

param.type: std::optional< std::function< bool(const Nfa &, const Nfa &, const StateSet &, const StateSet &, const State, const Nfa &)> >

briefdescription.para: Compute a language difference as nfa_included \ nfa_excluded.

detaileddescription.para: Computed as a lazy intersection of nfa_included and a complement of nfa_excluded. The NFAs are lazily determinized and the complement is constructed lazily as well, guided by nfa_included.

parameterdescription.para: NFA to include in the difference.
NFA to exclude from the difference.

Callback event handler for discovering a new macrostate in the language difference automaton for the first time. Return true if the computation should continue, and false if the computation should stop and return only the NFA for the language difference constructed so far. The parameters are: const Nfa& nfa_included, const Nfa& nfa_excluded, const StateSet& macrostate_included_state_set, const StateSet& macrostate_excluded_state_set, const State macrostate, const Nfa& nfa_lang_difference.

xrefdescription.para: : TODO: Add support for specifying first epsilon symbol and compute epsilon closure during determinization.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Nfa &lhs, const Nfa &rhs, const Symbol first_epsilon=EPSILON, std::unordered_map< std::pair< State, State >, State > *prod_map=nullptr)
memberdef.name: intersection

briefdescription.para: Compute intersection of two NFAs.

detaileddescription.para: Both automata can contain ε-transitions. The product preserves the ε-transitions, i.e., for each each product state (s, t) withs -ε-> p, (s, t) -ε-> (p, t) is created, and vice versa.
Automata must share alphabets. //TODO: this is not implemented yet.

parameterdescription.para: First NFA to compute intersection for.
Second NFA to compute intersection for.
smallest epsilon. //TODO: this should eventually be taken from the alphabet as anything larger than the largest symbol?
Mapping of pairs of the original states (lhs_state, rhs_state) to new product states (not used internally, allocated only when !=nullptr, expensive).

simplesect.para: NFA as a product of NFAs lhs and rhs with ε-transitions preserved.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Nfa &lhs, const Nfa &rhs, bool use_epsilon=false, StateRenaming *lhs_state_renaming=nullptr, StateRenaming *rhs_state_renaming=nullptr)
memberdef.name: concatenate

briefdescription.para: Concatenate two NFAs.

detaileddescription.para:
Supports epsilon symbols when use_epsilon is set to true.
lhs
First automaton to concatenate.
rhs
Second automaton to concatenate.
use_epsilon
Whether to concatenate over epsilon symbol.
lhs_state_renaming
Map mapping lhs states to result states.
rhs_state_renaming
Map mapping rhs states to result states.
Concatenated automaton.

briefdescription.para: Compute automaton accepting complement of aut.

parameterdescription.para: Automaton whose complement to compute.
Alphabet used for complementation.

Optional parameters to control the complementation algorithm:
"algorithm":
"classical": The classical algorithm determinizes the automaton, makes it complete, and swaps final and non-final states.
"brzozowski": The Brzozowski algorithm determinizes the automaton using Brzozowski minimization, makes it complete, and swaps final and non-final states.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Nfa &aut, const utils::OrdVector< Symbol > &symbols, const ParameterMap &params={ { "algorithm", "classical" } })
memberdef.name: complement

briefdescription.para: Compute automaton accepting complement of aut.

detaileddescription.para: This overloaded version complements over an already created ordered set of symbols instead of an alphabet. This is a more efficient solution in case you already have symbols precomputed or want to complement multiple automata over the same set of symbols: the function does not need to compute the ordered set of symbols from the alphabet again (and for each automaton).

parameterdescription.para: Automaton whose complement to compute.
Symbols to complement over.

Optional parameters to control the complementation algorithm:
"algorithm":
"classical": The classical algorithm determinizes the automaton, makes it complete, and swaps final and non-final states.
"brzozowski": The Brzozowski algorithm determinizes the automaton using Brzozowski minimization, makes it complete, and swaps final and non-final states.

briefdescription.para: Compute minimal deterministic automaton.

parameterdescription.para: Automaton whose minimal version to compute.

Optional parameters to control the minimization algorithm:
"algorithm": "brzozowski"

memberdef.argsstring: (const Nfa &aut, std::unordered_map< StateSet, State > *subset_map=nullptr, std::optional< std::function< bool(const Nfa &, const State, const StateSet &)> > macrostate_discover=std::nullopt)
memberdef.name: determinize

param.type: std::optional< std::function< bool(const Nfa &, const State, const StateSet &)> >

briefdescription.para: Determinize automaton.

parameterdescription.para: Automaton to determinize.
Map that maps sets of states of input automaton to states of determinized automaton.

Callback event handler for discovering a new macrostate for the first time. The parameters are the determinized NFA constructed so far, the current macrostate, and the set of the original states corresponding to the macrostate. Return true if the determinization should continue, and false if the determinization should stop and return only the determinized NFA constructed so far.

xrefdescription.para: : TODO: Add support for specifying first epsilon symbol and compute epsilon closure during determinization.

briefdescription.para: Reduce the size of the automaton.

parameterdescription.para: Automaton to reduce.
Mapping of original states to reduced states.

Optional parameters to control the reduction algorithm:
"algorithm": "simulation", "residual", and options to parametrize residual reduction, not utilized in simulation
"type": "after", "with",
"direction": "forward", "backward".

memberdef.argsstring: (const Nfa &smaller, const Nfa &bigger, Run *cex, const Alphabet *alphabet=nullptr, const ParameterMap &params={{ "algorithm", "antichains" }})
memberdef.name: is_included

briefdescription.para: Checks inclusion of languages of two NFAs: smaller and bigger (smaller <= bigger).

parameterdescription.para: First automaton to concatenate.
Second automaton to concatenate.
Counterexample for the inclusion.
Alphabet of both NFAs to compute with.

Optional parameters to control the equivalence check algorithm:
"algorithm": "naive", "antichains" (Default: "antichains")

simplesect.para: True if smaller is included in bigger, false otherwise.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Nfa &smaller, const Nfa &bigger, const Alphabet *const alphabet=nullptr, const ParameterMap &params={{ "algorithm", "antichains" }})
memberdef.name: is_included

briefdescription.para: Checks inclusion of languages of two NFAs: smaller and bigger (smaller <= bigger).

parameterdescription.para: First automaton to concatenate.
Second automaton to concatenate.
Alphabet of both NFAs to compute with.

Optional parameters to control the equivalence check algorithm:
"algorithm": "naive", "antichains" (Default: "antichains")

simplesect.para: True if smaller is included in bigger, false otherwise.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Nfa &lhs, const Nfa &rhs, const Alphabet *alphabet, const ParameterMap &params={{ "algorithm", "antichains"}})
memberdef.name: are_equivalent

briefdescription.para: Perform equivalence check of two NFAs: lhs and rhs.

parameterdescription.para: First automaton to concatenate.
Second automaton to concatenate.
Alphabet of both NFAs to compute with.

Optional parameters to control the equivalence check algorithm:
"algorithm": "naive", "antichains" (Default: "antichains")

simplesect.para: True if lhs and rhs are equivalent, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Perform equivalence check of two NFAs: lhs and rhs.

detaileddescription.para: The current implementation of Nfa does not accept input alphabet. For this reason, an alphabet has to be created from all transitions each time an operation on alphabet is called. When calling this function, the alphabet has to be computed first.

Hence, this function is less efficient than its alternative taking already defined alphabet as its parameter. That way, alphabet has to be computed only once, as opposed to the current ad-hoc construction of the alphabet. The use of the alternative with defined alphabet should be preferred.

parameterdescription.para: First automaton to concatenate.
Second automaton to concatenate.

Optional parameters to control the equivalence check algorithm:
"algorithm": "naive", "antichains" (Default: "antichains")

simplesect.para: True if lhs and rhs are equivalent, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Encodes a vector of strings (each corresponding to one symbol) into a Word instance.
Get the set of symbols to work with during operations.

parameterdescription.para: Optional alphabet shared between NFAs passed as an argument to a function.

briefdescription.para: Get any arbitrary accepted word in the language difference of nfa_included without nfa_excluded.

detaileddescription.para: The language difference automaton is lazily constructed without computing the whole determinized automata and the complememt of nfa_excluded. The algorithm returns an arbitrary word from the language difference constructed until the first macrostate with a final state in the original states in nfa_included and without any corresponding final states in nfa_excluded is encountered.

simplesect.para: The automaton does not contain any epsilon transitions.

parameterdescription.para: NFA to include in the language difference.
NFA to exclude in the language difference. TODO: Support lazy epsilon closure?

simplesect.para: An arbitrary word from the language difference, or std::nullopt if the language difference automaton is universal on the set of symbols from transitions of nfa_included.
